title: Carbon Fibre Morph Reactor
description: A morphing cube reactor sits at the center of a carbon fibre containment shell. The user must regulate reactor energy by adjusting morph intensity, shell rotation speed, and emission color across three coupled channels while obeying a total energy conservation law. Full undo/redo history tracks every parameter change.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net). Load AnimatedMorphCube.glb at the scene origin and CarbonFibre.glb scaled to form a surrounding containment shell (scale ~3x, centered on the cube). Set up a PerspectiveCamera at (0,3,6) with OrbitControls. Add an AmbientLight(0x334455) and two PointLights (warm and cool) orbiting the reactor.

Implement a three-channel energy system: Channel A controls morph target influence (0-1) on the AnimatedMorphCube, Channel B controls the carbon fibre shell's Y-axis rotation speed (0-2 rad/s), and Channel C controls a PointLight emission intensity (0-3). Enforce a conservation law: A + B + C must always equal a constant total of 3.0. When the user increases one channel via its slider, the other two decrease proportionally to maintain the sum.

Add an HTML overlay panel with: three labeled range sliders (A, B, C), a digital readout showing current values and their sum, an Undo button and a Redo button. Every slider change pushes a state snapshot {A, B, C} onto an undo stack (max 50 entries). Undo restores the previous state and pushes current to redo stack. Redo reverses this. Disable buttons when stacks are empty.

Additional interactions: clicking the morph cube toggles its wireframe mode (counts as an undoable action storing wireframe boolean in state). Double-clicking the carbon fibre shell cycles its material between three roughness presets (0.1, 0.5, 0.9), also undoable. A reset button restores defaults (A=1, B=1, C=1, wireframe off, roughness 0.5) and clears history.

Animate the morph cube using its embedded animation clip scaled by Channel A's value. Rotate the carbon fibre shell each frame by Channel B * deltaTime. Tint both point lights' color by mapping Channel C to a blue-to-orange gradient. Render with tone mapping (ACESFilmic) and antialiasing enabled. The scene should feel like a sci-fi reactor control interface.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  channelA: number,         // morph target influence on AnimatedMorphCube (0-1)
  channelB: number,         // carbon fibre shell Y-axis rotation speed in rad/s (0-2)
  channelC: number,         // point light emission intensity (0-3)
  energySum: number,         // sum of channelA + channelB + channelC, must equal 3.0
  undoStackSize: number,         // current number of entries in undo history stack
  redoStackSize: number,         // current number of entries in redo history stack
  shellRotationY: number,         // current Y-axis rotation angle of carbon fibre shell in radians
  morphCubeLoaded: boolean,         // true when AnimatedMorphCube.glb is loaded and ready
  carbonFibreLoaded: boolean,         // true when CarbonFibre.glb is loaded and ready
  totalStateChanges: number,         // cumulative count of all slider adjustments made
  conservationViolations: number,         // count of times energySum deviated from 3.0 by more than 0.01
  lastActionType: string,         // last user action - 'slider', 'undo', or 'redo'
}
